event_blocks.summary: José Mourinho, with a serious expression, declared his arrogance and status as a "special one," a "European champion," while sponsor logos like 'Fly Emirates' were visible. The narrative then depicted him in confrontational moments, described by a narrator as "the biggest ego amongst football managers" and "a one-man army against the world." The story shifted to Mourinho's beginnings at FC Porto, detailing his father Félix Mourinho's career as a goalkeeper and manager, and how young José learned football's cruelty. Early struggles, including his father's sacking on Christmas Day, strengthened Mourinho Jr., who realized he lacked the physical attributes to be a top player, leading him to pursue management. His journey began with "baby steps" and "any job possible" in football, leading to an unexpected opportunity as Bobby Robson's translator for Sporting Lisbon due to Robson's inability to speak the language. Robson lauded Mourinho's "first-class" tactical analysis, despite Mourinho being in his early thirties with no prior coaching experience. After gaining confidence with Robson, Mourinho became an assistant manager, achieving significant success with FC Porto, including "2 Portuguese Championships," "3 Super Cups," "1 Portuguese Cup," and reaching the "UCL SEMI FINALS." He then moved to FC Barcelona in 1996, initially facing resistance but supported by Robson. Mourinho evolved from a translator to offering his own tactical views, challenging the perceived incompatibility of these roles. During his time at Barcelona, he worked alongside stars like Pep Guardiola, with whom he shared a close initial relationship, though the narrator hinted at future "big fights over football's main stage."

José Mourinho.description: Appears in multiple forms: first in a suit at a press conference with microphones, then holding a trophy with a medal, and later in a blue tracksuit with red and white stripes, smiling and interacting with others. The Narrator describes his evolution from manager's assistant at FC Porto, achieving multiple championships and cup wins, to joining FC Barcelona where he developed his 'own tactical and football views' beyond just translating.

Félix Mourinho.description: Shown in a vintage photo wearing a green-and-white striped jersey, identified as part of the FC Porto youth team. The narrator describes him as Mourinho's father, a goalkeeper who played almost 300 games, was capped by Portugal, and later became a top-flight manager.

He speaks directly to the camera in a clear male voice, narrating the story of Mourinho's beginnings and career progression, accompanied by subtle background music.

The Narrator quotes his high praise for Mourinho's 'tactical analysis' and 'first-class' reports, expressing surprise at Mourinho's talent despite his lack of playing or coaching experience, and mentioning his need for a translator because he 'couldn't speak the language.'

The Narrator identifies him as one of the 'stars of the team' when Mourinho was at Barcelona, noting that their later rivalry had not yet developed.

raw_segments: The video opens with José Mourinho, dressed in a dark suit and light blue shirt, speaking into microphones with a serious expression. His distinct voice declares, "I'm sorry I'm a bit arrogant, we have a top manager," followed by "Again, please don't call me arrogant because what I'm saying is true. I'm European champion, so I'm not one of the bottle. I think I'm a special one," with these bold statements also appearing as on-screen text. Sponsor logos like 'Fly Emirates,' 'Barclaycard,' and 'Umbro' are visible in the background. The scene transitions to a montage of Mourinho in various confrontational moments—arguing with officials, clashing with other managers, and walking off the pitch—as a male narrator with a clear, engaging voice describes him as "the biggest ego amongst football managers" and "a one-man army against the world," while bold text overlays like 'BIGGEST EGO' and 'THE STORY OF JOSÉ MOURINHO AGAINST THE WORLD' reinforce the narrative. The video then shifts to the narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio with football jerseys and a world map backdrop. He introduces "The Beginnings: FC Porto," explaining that Mourinho's love for the game started before he was born. Archival photos appear, showing a young Mourinho in a black-and-white striped jersey as part of a youth team, highlighted by a red circle, and his father, Félix Mourinho, in a green-and-white striped jersey. The narrator details Félix's career as a goalkeeper, playing almost "300 GAMES" and being capped by Portugal, before becoming a top-flight manager. The segment ends with a close-up of a young, intense Mourinho at a press conference, with the word 'CRUEL' overlaid, as the narrator states that José "soon learned just how cruel football can be."

The video opens with a vintage team photo of FC Porto's youth squad, highlighting a young José Mourinho in a black-and-white striped jersey, seated in the front row with a serious expression, as the Narrator's clear, confident male voice begins to tell his story over slightly dramatic background music. The Narrator, seen in a studio with a world map and football jerseys, explains that 'Heel off fired Jose's dad and left a scar for his young boy,' adding that 'Jose learned one of football and life's most painful lessons that day.' Archival footage shows a young Mourinho in a similar jersey, followed by a black-and-white image of him in a dark suit, suggesting a shift in his life path. The Narrator continues, emphasizing Mourinho’s early struggles, including his father being sacked on Christmas Day, illustrated by a blurred image of a dejected man. The Narrator recounts, 'One day you're the happy son of a coach skipper, and the next day, you find your dad crying on Christmas Day,' but notes these events 'gave strength to Mourinho Jr.' The video then cuts to a close-up of a young Mourinho with intense eyes, overlaid with text stating 'I DIDN’T HAVE WHAT IT TAKE,' as the Narrator explains, 'He realized he didn't have what it takes,' lacking the 'proper pace and power to be a top player.' The scene shifts to a modern football stadium, where Mourinho, now in a dark tracksuit, walks confidently across the pitch, surrounded by staff and players, symbolizing his rise after deciding 'to be a top manager.' The video ends with the Narrator in the studio, gesturing as he speaks, with on-screen text highlighting Mourinho’s decision to quit business school on the first day and pursue sports science, underscoring his pivotal career choice.

The segment opens with a male Narrator, with reddish hair and a mustache, speaking in a clear, informative voice from a studio adorned with football jerseys, a world map, and decorative plants. He begins recounting José Mourinho's early career, describing his initial 'baby steps' and willingness to take 'any job possible' in football. The scene transitions to vintage black-and-white footage of a young Mourinho, highlighted by a red circle, wearing an FC Porto youth jersey, as the Narrator explains his unexpected opportunity arose when Bobby Robson, seen in archival footage at a press conference with a Newcastle United crest behind him, needed a translator for Sporting Lisbon because he 'couldn't speak the language.' The Narrator details how Mourinho accepted this 'unexpected job' as Robson's translator, a 'win-win situation' where he got to work alongside a 'top-class manager' and learn 'about football and tactics.' The Narrator then quotes Robson's profound praise for Mourinho's 'tactical analysis,' stating, 'He'd come back and hand me a dossier that was absolutely first class. I mean: first class. As good as anything I’ve ever received,' and marveling that Mourinho, 'in his early thirties, never been a player, never been a coach to speak of either,' provided such exceptional reports. The video cuts back to the Narrator, who continues to explain Mourinho's rise, followed by a montage showing Mourinho's evolution: confidently walking in a dark tracksuit, addressing a press conference surrounded by microphones, and gesturing animatedly during a match in a white and blue striped shirt. The segment concludes with the Narrator summarizing Mourinho's early achievements, displayed as bold text: '2 Portuguese Championships, 3 Super Cups, 1 Portuguese Cup,' capturing an informative and nostalgic atmosphere.

The minute opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, standing in his football memorabilia-adorned studio, speaking directly to the camera. A subtle background music track accompanies his narration as he explains how José Mourinho, after gaining confidence with Bobby, became the manager's assistant, achieving '2 PORTUGUESE CHAMPIONSHIPS,' '3 SUPER CUPS,' '1 PORTUGUESE CUP,' and reaching the 'UCL SEMI FINALS' with FC Porto, as on-screen text highlights these successes. The scene transitions to archival footage of a man in a blue tracksuit with red and white stripes, identified as Pep Guardiola, sitting on the FC Barcelona bench, with 'FC BARCELONA' text appearing, as the Narrator reveals Mourinho's move to the club in 1996. The Narrator returns, detailing how Barcelona initially didn't need Mourinho and wanted to push him out, but Robson insisted. This is visually followed by a press conference scene where José Mourinho, in a suit, is surrounded by microphones (including 'COPE' and 'SAD'), illustrating his public role, and then a celebratory moment where he holds a trophy with a medal alongside another man in a Kappa tracksuit. The Narrator continues, explaining how Mourinho evolved from merely translating to answering with his 'own tactical and football views,' challenging the belief that the assistant coach and translator roles were incompatible. The video then cuts to a close-up of Mourinho smiling, followed by archival footage showing Mourinho in a blue tracksuit with red and white stripes, with the text 'PEP GUARDIOLA' overlaid, as the Narrator identifies Pep Guardiola as one of the team's stars. A subsequent shot of two men in similar tracksuits with 'BFFs' text appears, visually emphasizing their initial close relationship, which the Narrator clarifies wasn't yet a rivalry, before hinting at future 'big fights over football's main stage' as the segment ends with him back in the studio.

event_blocks.summary: The Narrator, situated in a studio featuring a world map, football jerseys, and plants, introduces José Mourinho's trajectory, explaining his ambition beyond assisting at Barcelona. Archival footage depicts a young Mourinho in a tracksuit at a press conference, identified as 'SITTING IN BENFICA’S BIG CHAIR,' with the Narrator detailing his brief 2000 head coach tenure at Benfica, cut short after nine games by a presidential change. The Narrator emphasizes Mourinho's deep understanding of football's volatility, noting his subsequent year without a club before taking the helm at União de Leiria, where he secured a fifth-place finish. The narrative shifts to a man in a suit being escorted, accompanied by the text 'NEW PORTO HEAD COACH,' as the Narrator announces Mourinho's appointment as Porto's new head coach in early 2002. Footage shows Mourinho, now in a striped tracksuit, smiling and engaging, signifying his pivotal new role. Returning to the studio, the Narrator, holding a card proclaiming 'FC PORTO: THE REAL BIRTH OF THE SPECIAL ONE,' passionately asserts that Mourinho revitalized the struggling club, fundamentally altering its fortunes.

event_blocks.summary: Grainy archival footage shows José Mourinho, in a blue tracksuit, clapping at a stadium as the Narrator explains how Mourinho guided FC Porto to 3rd place in the league, losing only two games, and laying the foundations for a season where he would "win, well, everything." The scene transitions to the Narrator in a studio setting before a backdrop of football jerseys and a world map, speaking into a microphone with expressive hand gestures. Celebratory images of FC Porto players lifting trophies, with on-screen text detailing their successes: Domestic League, Portuguese Cup, and UEFA Europa League, accompany the Narrator's emphasis that this was Mourinho's "first ever treble," making Porto "Portugal's strongest team internationally as no other since the 80s." The Narrator reappears in the studio, his voice underscoring Mourinho's role as a 'Mastermind' with dynamic text effects, stating that such a manager "doesn't stop there" and aims for "the top prize." Mourinho is then seen at a press conference, wearing a blue FC Porto jacket, confidently delivering a bold statement in Portuguese: "In normal conditions, we’ll be champions. And in abnormal conditions... we’ll be champions as well!" amidst colorful microphones from 'COPE' and 'SAD'. Footage follows of Mourinho and his staff celebrating wildly on the pitch, with the text 'Winning the Champions League' displayed, as the Narrator explains that in 2004, Mourinho "made sure that the world really got to know him" by winning the Champions League, overcoming teams like Deportivo La Coruña, Lyon, Manchester United, and Monaco. The scene shifts to Mourinho in a red and white tracksuit, looking dejected, before transitioning to a close-up of him in a blue tracksuit, smiling and interacting with others. The Narrator declares Mourinho "eternal," and begins to recount a "heated confrontation" with "legendary coach Sir Alex" during their tie against Manchester United. The video concludes with a shot of Mourinho and Bobby Robson sharing a moment of camaraderie, as the Narrator returns to the studio to wrap up the segment, the warm studio lighting contrasting with the vintage, grainy archival footage.

event_blocks.summary: The Narrator, positioned in a studio decorated with a world map and football jerseys, recounts José Mourinho's pivotal touchline celebration at Old Trafford after a Porto goal, an act that announced his arrival on the global stage and foreshadowed his subsequent career move. Following his success in Portugal and the conclusion of his contract with Porto, Mourinho transitioned to London, signing with Chelsea Football Club and becoming the first Portuguese manager in the Premier League. This move was visually underscored by scenes of the Manchester United store, a Times Square crowd, and an inset of Mourinho being embraced, symbolizing public recognition. Archival footage then depicted Mourinho's stylish arrival at Stamford Bridge on July 2, 2004, accompanied by '#PORTUGANGSTA' and upbeat music. The narrative shifts to a memorable press conference, where Mourinho, surrounded by numerous media microphones, declared with characteristic confidence, "I'm sorry I'm a bit arrogant. We have a top manager. Again, don't please don't call me arrogant because what I'm saying is true. I'm European Champion. So, I'm not...", a statement reinforced by on-screen text emphasizing his status as 'European Champion' and not 'one of the bottom.' The video concludes with the Narrator back in the studio.

event_blocks.summary: José Mourinho, in a press conference, declared himself "a special one," a sentiment echoed by on-screen text and the Narrator, who detailed his "unique character" and "special" status, evidenced by the 2005 Carling Cup Final victory over Liverpool (3-2), captured in celebration footage of Chelsea players and fans. Mourinho's "shush" gesture towards Liverpool fans at Anfield, following their chants against him, was highlighted, with on-screen text stating, 'THEY DIDN’T KNOW WHO THEY WERE DEALING WITH.' His Champions League performances were also cited as proof of his specialness, despite being "prohibited from being in the stadium" for a quarter-final match against Bayern Munich due to "indiscretions," as confirmed by a graphic overlay. Later, after Liverpool fans gained revenge by beating Chelsea in the 2005 Champions League semi-finals with a questionable Luis Garcia goal, eliminating Mourinho's team from Europe, Chelsea players, including Drogba, celebrated winning the English Premier League title, ending a 50-year drought. The Narrator emphasized the defensive prowess of that Chelsea team, featuring stars like Drogba, Essien, Lampard, Duff, Terry, Gallas, Ferreira, and Carvalho, who conceded a record-low 15 goals in the Premier League season. The segment concluded with footage of Mourinho holding a trophy, signifying Chelsea's win of the Community Shield, starting the following season with another trophy.

raw_segments: The video opens with José Mourinho, in a dark suit at a press conference, confidently declaring in a slightly muffled voice, "I'm not one of the bottom. I think I'm a special one," with on-screen text reinforcing his statement: 'I’m European Champion, I am not one of the bottom.' The scene transitions to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio with football memorabilia. He speaks directly to the camera with a clear, articulate voice, asking rhetorically, "Arrogant? Yeah, possibly. Special one? Absolutely, zero doubt about it. You are and always have been special, Jose." The Narrator continues, explaining how "Mourinho had become the special one," before the video cuts to archival footage of a football match. A scoreboard displays 'CARLING CUP FINAL 2005' with Chelsea defeating Liverpool 3-2, followed by jubilant celebration footage of Chelsea players and fans, described by the Narrator as "an epic 3-2 victory against Liverpool." The Narrator reappears, explaining that this was when "the English media properly caught a glimpse of the special one's unique character." The video then shows Mourinho in a green jacket, making a 'shush' gesture with his finger to his lips, looking straight at the Liverpool fans, as the Narrator explains that "the fans at Anfield started to chant against him, and clearly, they didn't know who they were dealing with." On-screen text reinforces this: 'THEY DIDN’T KNOW WHO THEY WERE DEALING WITH.' The Narrator continues, stating Mourinho's Champions League performances "also proved that he was special." The scene shifts to a quarter-final match against Bayern Munich, showing Mourinho in a blue tracksuit, as the Narrator reveals he "was prohibited from being in the stadium" due to "indiscretions." A red-and-blue graphic with text 'EARNED A BAN FROM THE STADIUM' and 'INDISCRETIONS' visually confirms this disciplinary incident. The segment concludes with the Narrator back in the studio, continuing his story about Mourinho, stating, "He snuck into the Chelsea dressing room seven hours before..." all set against a backdrop of background music and occasional crowd noise.

The segment opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera from a studio adorned with a world map, football jerseys, and plants. He introduces a segment on Chelsea FC's history, stating, "This guy really is a piece of work. And if you were surprised by that, check this out." The scene cuts to archival footage of a dejected man in a grey sweater, symbolizing emotional distress, as the Narrator continues, "After the team talk, he left the stadium in a laundry basket. Unbelievable stuff. Classic Jose Mourinho." This transitions to a crowd of Liverpool fans celebrating, one holding a sign reading 'BUYING THIS ONE, CHELSKI!', with on-screen text 'LIVERPOOL BEAT CHELSEA IN THE 2005 UCL SEMI FINALS'. The Narrator explains, "Liverpool fans did get their revenge with Mourinho as they beat Chelsea in the 2005 Champions League semi-finals with a very questionable goal from Luis Garcia." He adds, "Mourinho's team were eliminated from Europe. But the sadness didn't last long." The video then shows Chelsea players in blue kits, including Drogba, celebrating a Premier League title, lifting the trophy, with text overlays 'LED HIS TEAM TO WIN THE TOP-FLIGHT DOMESTIC TITLE' and 'ENGLISH PREMIER LEAGUE'. The Narrator confirms, "He then led his team to win the top-flight domestic title that was denied from the Blues for half a century. The English Premier League. After over 50 years, Mourinho's Chelsea lifted the trophy with a team full of stars like Drogba, Essien, Lampard, Duff, but a major part of their success was down to their defense." The Narrator reappears, holding a blue Chelsea jersey with 'LAMAR' and the number 8, before a close-up of a player in a blue jersey with 'DROGBA 11' on the back. The Narrator continues, "The likes of John Terry, William Gallas, Ferreira, and Carvalho led in just 15 goals in that Premier League season. A record that still stands to this day." On-screen text reinforces '15 GOALS IN A PREMIER LEAGUE SEASON' and 'A RECORD THAT STILL STANDS'. The final segment shows the Narrator again, with text 'STARTED THE FOLLOWING SEASON WITH ANOTHER TROPHY', followed by footage of José Mourinho in a suit, holding a trophy, with text 'WON THE COMMUNITY SHIELD' overlaid. The Narrator concludes, "Chelsea couldn't have been any happier with their new manager. The London side started the following season with another trophy as Mourinho's squad..."

event_blocks.summary: The Narrator, situated in a studio decorated with a world map and football jerseys, recounts Chelsea FC's triumphant second season under 'the special one,' José Mourinho, highlighting their "WON THE COMMUNITY SHIELD" and "FIRST PLACE IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE" victories, marking their fourth consecutive domestic title. Archival footage depicts Chelsea players and fans celebrating with a trophy, a jubilant parade featuring a 'BACK-TO-BACK CHAMPIONS 2005-2006' banner, and a 3-0 win against Manchester United, with Drogba seen celebrating. Mourinho, described as 'the king of England' with the text overlay 'HE WAS THE KING OF ENGLAND', is shown smiling confidently as he throws his blazer and medals to the crowd, having also secured the League Cup and FA Cup, evidenced by a Carling Cup Final 2005 scoreboard showing Chelsea 3, Liverpool 2. The narrative is briefly interrupted by a comedic cutaway of a man in a yellow patterned shirt and a muffled voice asking, 'I'm sorry, could you speak into my good ear?', before the Narrator returns to detail Mourinho's arrest due to a dog quarantine incident. The scene then shifts to Mourinho exiting a house under the text 'MUTUAL CONSENT,' signaling his departure, and concluding with him at a press conference, wearing a black leather jacket, declaring, "The decision was made, Chelsea look forward, I look forward. They move on, I move on. I'll keep winning important things... They'll keep winning something," with a slight laugh.

His actual voice is heard with a distinct Portuguese accent, delivering a confident and resolute message about moving on from Chelsea, stating 'The decision was made, Chelsea look forward, I look forward. They move on, I move on.' and 'I’ll keep winning important things... They’ll keep winning something (LAUGHS)'.

raw_segments: The minute opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking in a clear, engaging voice from a studio adorned with a world map and football jerseys. Archival footage of Chelsea FC celebrating a major victory plays, showing players and fans lifting a trophy amidst a sea of blue, accompanied by bold on-screen text declaring 'WON THE COMMUNITY SHIELD' and 'FIRST PLACE IN THE PREMIER LEAGUE'. The Narrator explains how 'the special one' had taken England by assault, detailing Chelsea's second season bringing another first place in the Premier League, marking their fourth consecutive domestic title. A jubilant parade scene follows, featuring a large banner reading 'BACK-TO-BACK CHAMPIONS 2005-2006' and on-screen text 'FOURTH CONSECUTIVE DOMESTIC TITLE IN A ROW'. The Narrator continues, describing how Chelsea lifted the Premier League trophy after beating Manchester United 3-0, with visuals of players like Drogba celebrating. A close-up of José Mourinho in a suit, smiling confidently, appears with the text 'HE WAS THE KING OF ENGLAND', as the Narrator recounts Mourinho throwing his blazer and medals to the crowd, emphasizing his passion. The Narrator adds that Mourinho also won the League Cup and FA Cup, beating Arsenal and Manchester United, while a Carling Cup Final 2005 scoreboard (Chelsea 3, Liverpool 2) and a trophy celebration scene with Mourinho are shown. The Narrator declares Mourinho 'the king of England,' stating everything was going perfectly for the Portuguese tactician, who had won every domestic title again. This narrative is abruptly interrupted by a comedic sound effect and a muffled voice asking, 'I'm sorry, could you speak into my good ear?', coinciding with a brief cutaway of a man in a yellow patterned shirt making a dramatic gesture. The Narrator then returns, expressing surprise ('Wait, what?') before explaining that Mourinho spent time at the police department due to his dog, which was supposed to be in quarantine. The studio backdrop, with its warm lighting and decorative plants, remains consistent throughout the Narrator's segments.

The minute opens with a Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, in a studio adorned with football memorabilia, speaking directly to the camera. He recounts a dramatic story, accompanied by light, suspenseful music, about José Mourinho. Archival footage shows Mourinho in a Chelsea FC tracksuit with 'SAMSUNG' branding at a press conference, as red text overlays quote him describing how he secretly rescued a small dog from quarantine by putting it in a backpack and jumping out a window. Silhouetted police officers are briefly seen, adding to the tension. The scene then shifts to Mourinho in a suit, celebrating a trophy with a medal, followed by jubilant Chelsea fans in blue attire celebrating in a stadium and on the streets, waving flags, as the Narrator concludes the dog story, mentioning Mourinho's arrest and the fans' campaign to free the dog. The Narrator reappears, introducing a shift in tone with the theme, "BUT FAMILIES CAN HURT YOU AS WELL." This leads to a scene of Mourinho exiting a house, with the text 'MUTUAL CONSENT' displayed, signaling his departure. The video culminates with Mourinho at another press conference, wearing a black leather jacket against a backdrop of sponsor logos like Heineken and Ford. His actual voice, with a confident Portuguese accent, is heard for the first time amidst subtle press conference ambient sounds, as he declares, "The decision was made, Chelsea look forward, I look forward. They move on, I move on." Red text overlays reinforce his words as he defiantly adds, "I'll keep winning important things... They'll keep winning something," ending with a slight laugh and a composed expression, emphasizing his resilience.

event_blocks.summary: José Mourinho, clad in a black leather jacket, confidently addressed a press conference against a backdrop of sponsor logos, stating, "They won an FA Cup," as on-screen text confirmed. Amidst a consistent, slightly dramatic instrumental score, the scene shifted to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, situated in a studio adorned with football jerseys and a world map. With expressive hand gestures, he explained that "Mutual consent is a way of saying a lot of disagreements between the Portuguese manager and the chairman Roman Abramovich were reported." He then posited that Mourinho's next move "would be Barcelona," adding, "Yes, before moving to Italy, Mourinho had his eyes set on a glorious return to Barça," a statement underscored by archival footage of Mourinho and Abramovich shaking hands, overlaid with text about their disagreements, followed by a red-tinted FC Barcelona crest and a dramatic shot of Mourinho with the text 'GLORIOUS RETURN TO BARÇA.' The Narrator reappeared, questioning if "the Barcelona board keen on signing a former man of the house? The story goes that Mourinho had a meeting with the board that lasted several hours." The video then cut to Mourinho in an FC Barcelona training kit, gesturing during a match, with the text 'WAS HIM WHO EXPECTED THE,' as the Narrator detailed how Mourinho "presented his analysis of the squad, the strengths, the weaknesses, who needed to stay, who needed to go. The board was said to have been impressed and convinced, but the rest is not clear." Returning to the studio, the Narrator emphatically stated, "Mourinho himself said it was him who rejected the job and that maybe one day he'll explain the situation in detail." A close-up of a man in a striped shirt appeared with the text 'ONE PERSON,' as the Narrator revealed, "Another version of this story says that when things were going to be final, one person single-handedly tore it all down." Archival footage then showed Johan Cruyff, an older man in a blue tracksuit, pointing authoritatively, with his name displayed, as the Narrator identified him as "None other than Johan Cruyff, who allegedly told the board that the Portuguese coach wasn't the right guy for the job." The Narrator concluded, followed by footage of Pep Guardiola in a blue tracksuit on the FC Barcelona bench, with the text 'PEP GUARDIOLA' overlaid, that the board "would do much better with an inexperienced Pep Guardiola at the helm of the Blaugrana." The segment ended with the Narrator speaking directly to the camera, maintaining the studio’s warm, thematic lighting and decor, stating, "Now this..."

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with José Mourinho's perceived 'villain' origin, marked by a golden crown and a villainous cackle, as he vowed revenge after being hurt, taking his personality and experience to Inter Milan. His first season saw him win the Italian Super Cup and the Scudetto, but Inter fans, unsatisfied with Champions League performance compared to AC Milan's success, demanded international glory. Mourinho saw the departure of club legends as an opportunity to build his own team, making strategic signings like Milito, Sneijder, Motta, and Eto'o, a move described as a significant gamble. He then achieved Inter's first treble, winning Serie A, Coppa Italia, and the Champions League by defeating Bayern Munich 2-0, fulfilling a six-decade desire. Despite this triumph, Mourinho expressed sadness about potentially leaving Inter, a sentiment that may have dissipated upon his record-breaking move to Real Madrid with the objective of stopping Barcelona. His tenure at Real Madrid was marked by intense rivalry with Barcelona, fueled by clashes between Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi, and Mourinho's strategic battles with Pep Guardiola. Mourinho's public persona was characterized by controversial statements, including remarks about Guardiola's Champions League win and a public confrontation with referee Fernando Teixeira, where he accused the referee of 'screwing up professionals' and making decisions against Real Madrid. He also made a controversial statement about Pedro Leon and allegedly made a harsh remark to Mesut Özil regarding his girlfriend. The narrative highlights Mourinho's 'special one' persona, his 'peculiar press conferences' after losses, and his role in a period of unprecedented rivalry between Real Madrid and Barcelona, involving four Clásicos in 18 days.

José Mourinho.description: Referred to as 'the special one' and 'the Portuguese' by the Narrator, he is described as being 'visibly furious' and 'outraged' with the referee, shouting 'What an artist you are, how you like to screw up professionals.' He is later seen in a dark suit and tie at a press conference, speaking seriously in front of a 'CARLING CUP' backdrop, with microphones visible, indicating media attention. The Narrator states his anger was 'totally understandable.'

raw_segments: The video opens with a vibrant, animated sequence featuring a golden crown adorned with colorful jewels, symbolizing prestige and triumph, alongside the 'Soccer Stories' logo and a scoreboard highlighting 'Chelsea FC'. This visual is accompanied by dramatic, triumphant orchestral music and an unsettling, almost villainous cackle, as the Narrator states, "is how a super villain was born." The scene transitions to a close-up of José Mourinho’s face, superimposed over the crown, emphasizing his legendary status, as the Narrator explains, "Mourinho was hurt, and he would have his revenge." Mourinho is then seen seated on the Inter Milan bench, wearing a black jacket, with text overlays highlighting 'His Personality' and 'His Experience,' as the Narrator continues, "The special one took his personality and experience to Inter squad." A title card 'INTER MILAN: MOU'S MIRACLE' appears, followed by archival footage of Mourinho celebrating with Inter Milan players, including a goalkeeper in a grey kit, as text announces 'IN HIS 1ST SEASON HE WON THE ITALIAN SUPERCUP'. The Narrator confirms, "In his first season he won the Italian Super Cup and, like usual, the top flight Italian trophy, the Scudetto." The scene cuts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, in a studio with football memorabilia. He gestures as he discusses Mourinho’s achievements, with text overlays like 'PERFORMANCE IN THE UCL' and 'CITY RIVALS AC MILAN WIN FIVE UCL TROPHIES' appearing, while he explains that despite domestic success, Inter fans were unhappy with the team's "sour performance in the Champions League" and demanded "international success right away" after AC Milan's five UCL trophies. The video then shows Mourinho on a training ground, walking with another man in a puffer jacket, as text states 'MOURINHO SAW IT AS THE PERFECT CHANCE'. The Narrator elaborates that in his second season, "club legends Adriano, Cruz, and Crespo said goodbye," and Mourinho "saw it as the perfect chance to truly build a team of his own choosing." The final frames include a blurred shot of a player in an Inter Milan jersey, followed by a close-up of a player celebrating with a yellow corner flag in the background, reinforcing the theme of triumph and legacy. The Narrator concludes by detailing Mourinho's strategic signings, mentioning "Diego Milito, Schneider, Thiago Motta, and Samuel Eto'o," describing it as "one of their biggest gambles ever," before the audio cuts off as he discusses Eto'o's arrival.

The segment opens with a male Narrator, with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera from a studio adorned with a world map and football jerseys. His clear voice, accompanied by dramatic orchestral music, immediately sets a historical tone, discussing football figures like Zlatan Ibrahimović and Pep Guardiola. The scene transitions to archival footage of José Mourinho in a white Inter Milan tracksuit walking on the pitch during a Champions League final, with on-screen text highlighting 'CHAMPIONS LEAGUE FINAL STAGES'. The Narrator explains how Inter won 'everything,' achieving their first treble by beating Lazio in Serie A, winning the Coppa Italia, and ultimately becoming 'Kings of Europe' by defeating Bayern 2-0 in the final, lifting the Champions League trophy they had 'desired for almost six decades.' Visually, Inter Milan players in blue-and-black striped jerseys are seen celebrating, hoisting the trophy, and Mourinho is jubilantly lifted by his team. Mourinho then appears in a black jacket, celebrating with arms raised, as the Narrator details his re-encounter with the UEFA Champions League. The narrative shifts to the semi-finals, with text stating 'IN THE SEMI-FINALS HE FACED BARCELONA', led by Pep Guardiola and Messi. A dejected-looking Barcelona player in a pink jersey is shown in a close-up, contrasting with a brief, glitchy shot of Mourinho in a dark suit, suggesting tension. The segment concludes with a dramatic, blurred close-up of Mourinho’s intense face, as the Narrator mentions he was 'outraged for the controversial...' before the audio fades.

The video opens with a grainy, vintage-style close-up of a player in a yellow jersey, transitioning to a chaotic celebration scene with fans and players in a stadium, suggesting a historic victory. The Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, stands in his studio before a backdrop of football jerseys and a world map, speaking animatedly into a microphone. He recounts a 2006 knockout stage match where Barcelona progressed against Chelsea, despite an Argentine player allegedly exaggerating fouls. He explains how the Barcelona team, considered the best of all time, was surprised by Mourinho's strategies, leading to Inter's progression to the finals after a 3-1 victory and a 1-0 defeat. The narrative shifts to a dramatic Champions League final, showing José Mourinho in a black jacket celebrating wildly on the pitch, embracing players and staff, with the scoreboard reading 'FINAL MADRID 2010' and a 0-1 scoreline. The Narrator describes Mourinho "going crazy" and "running around the Camp Nou" in an "epic win and an even more epic celebration." The camera captures Mourinho’s emotional reaction, including a close-up of him with his hands on his head, followed by a wide shot of the stadium filled with fans. While Inter fans were still chanting his name, Mourinho is heard sadly saying, "Sad, as almost for sure it's my last game with Inter," a statement the Narrator confirms was made shortly after conquering Europe. The Narrator continues, noting Mourinho's sadness may have gone away when he signed with Real Madrid for a world record compensation fee, brought in with the mission "to stop Barcelona." The scene cuts to a press conference where Mourinho, in a white tracksuit, looks intense and focused. The video then transitions to a montage of Real Madrid’s struggles, including a dejected Cristiano Ronaldo in a white jersey, and a press conference where an older man in a dark suit (Florentino Pérez), identified by the Narrator as Real Madrid's president, speaks with the text 'THE WORST GAME EVER PLAYED BY THE WHITES' overlaid, referring to a 5-0 defeat against Barcelona. The segment ends with a brief shot of the Narrator, then a close-up of Mourinho in a blue tracksuit, followed by a final frame of the Narrator in his studio, maintaining the narrative thread.

The segment opens with a male Narrator, with reddish hair and a mustache, in a studio setting featuring a world map, hanging football jerseys, and decorative plants, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures over a dramatic, suspenseful background music. He narrates, explaining that while Mourinho called a previous defeat the biggest of his career, he always had something to say about Guardiola, claiming he'd be ashamed to win a UCL trophy the way Pep did in 2009. The scene then transitions to archival footage of José Mourinho, in a dark suit at a press conference with microphones visible, speaking with a serious expression. His voice is heard in Spanish, stating that Pep Guardiola is a 'fantastic football manager' but that he would be ashamed to win a Champions League title the way Guardiola did, due to the 'Stamford Bridge scandal.' On-screen text in bold red reinforces his controversial remarks, including 'I’D BE ASHAMED OF WINNING.' Mourinho continues, predicting a similar 'Santiago Bernabéu scandal' if Guardiola wins again. The video then cuts to intense match footage, accompanied by a 'fight' sound effect, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a white Real Madrid jersey and Lionel Messi in a blue-and-red FC Barcelona jersey in a heated confrontation. The Narrator's voice returns, describing how in 2011, El Clásico became the biggest game, with Barcelona and Real Madrid embarking on an 'unprecedented period' of rivalry. This is overlaid with large white and red text reading 'UNPRECEDENTED' and 'PLAYING FOUR CLÁSICOS IN 18 DAYS' as Mourinho and Guardiola are seen in a tense face-off during a press event. The Narrator details the four Clásicos in 18 days, including a La Liga match-up, a Copa del Rey final, and two Champions League semi-final legs, emphasizing 'Madrid v Barcelona,' 'Ronaldo v Messi,' and 'Mourinho v Pep.' The segment concludes with a quick montage of Mourinho walking through a tunnel in a Real Madrid tracksuit, with stylized, bold white text 'MOU' appearing, symbolizing his identity and influence, as the dramatic music continues.

The segment opens with an aerial view of a football pitch during a pre-match lineup, showing players from Inter Milan and Real Madrid walking towards the center, accompanied by referees and officials. A driving, slightly dramatic orchestral track plays as a male Narrator, speaking with a clear English accent, introduces the historical context, stating that while Guardiola was the treble winner of 2009, José Mourinho was "the king of 2010." The scene transitions to José Mourinho, first seen in a dark suit on the sidelines with a serious expression, then cutting to the Narrator in a studio setting, wearing a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures against a backdrop of a world map, football jerseys, and plants. The Narrator continues, explaining that Real Madrid's consolation was winning the Copa del Rey final, but Mourinho's public behavior in Madrid was "a little off." The video cuts to archival footage of Mourinho in a black Real Madrid tracksuit at a press conference, speaking intensely in front of sponsor logos like Heineken, Ford, and Adidas. On-screen text highlights his controversial remarks, including 'POKED TITO VILANOVA’S EYE', which the Narrator explicitly references, noting Mourinho's behavior was "more than just a little off." The Narrator further explains that Mourinho was known for not being soft with rivals or his own players, and was even reported to make Mesut Özil leave his girlfriend. This leads to an AI re-enactment showing a younger Mourinho in a blue denim shirt confronting an older man in a grey sweater, with text overlays quoting a dramatic, fatherly warning. The Narrator directly quotes Mourinho's alleged words to Özil: "Hey Özil, idiot, let me tell you something as if I were your father, because this girl you're going out with has slept with everyone at Inter and AC Milan, including the coaching staff of both teams." The Narrator continues his commentary with animated gestures before cutting back to Mourinho at the press conference, now with a microphone in front of him, his expression stern and focused, as the AI re-enactment continues, emphasizing the dramatic tone of the narrative.

The segment opens with a Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures from a studio featuring a world map and hanging football jerseys. His clear, English-accented voice, accompanied by dramatic, slightly tense instrumental background music, introduces a controversial statement by José Mourinho. The scene cuts to Mourinho, in a dark suit, at a press conference with sponsor logos like MasterCard and Ford behind him and microphones in front. He speaks in Spanish with a distinct accent, stating, "YOU’RE SPEAKING ABOUT PEDRO LEON AS IF HE’S ZIDANE OR MARADONA. PEDRO LEON IS AN EXCELLENT PLAYER, BUT ONLY TWO DAYS AGO, HE WAS PLAYING FOR GETAFE," with these exact quotes highlighted by red on-screen text. The video then shows Mourinho on the sidelines in a dark tracksuit, walking with a serious expression, followed by a close-up of him smiling while on a phone call, as the Narrator's voice returns to describe Mourinho's "special one" persona and his time at Real Madrid. The Narrator continues his commentary, leading into a match scene where Cristiano Ronaldo, in a white Real Madrid jersey, intensely competes against Lionel Messi, in a blue-and-red FC Barcelona jersey, both shown in close-up during a physical confrontation on the pitch, which the Narrator describes as a "heated Clásico." The segment concludes with the Narrator, gesturing in his consistent studio setting, explaining Mourinho's "peculiar press conferences" after losses, mentioning the referee and Pep Guardiola.

The segment opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera from a studio backdrop featuring a world map and football jerseys. He narrates, detailing how 'the special one,' José Mourinho, went to the referee Fernando Teixeira's car park after a match, confronting him for his actions during the game. The Narrator quotes Mourinho, shouting, 'What an artist you are, how you like to screw up professionals,' visibly furious. On-screen text confirms 'LUCKILY THINGS DIDN'T GO PHYSICAL' as the Narrator explains Mourinho's outrage. The narrative continues, highlighting 'CONTROVERSIAL DECISIONS IN THAT GAME,' with text emphasizing '*ALL* OF THEM AGAINST REAL MADRID.' A black-and-white, grainy shot of a shadowy figure on a wet surface briefly appears, symbolizing tension, before the Narrator returns to describe how the referee denied Real Madrid a penalty and a legal goal. A brief comedic cutaway shows a man in a yellow patterned shirt making an exaggerated gesture. The scene then shifts to Cristiano Ronaldo in a white Real Madrid jersey, arguing with a referee and later celebrating a goal with teammates. The Narrator reappears, stating 'MOURINHO'S ANGER WAS TOTALLY UNDERSTANDABLE,' as the referee is described as having prevented Real Madrid from executing a last free kick by calling full time. The segment concludes with José Mourinho at a press conference, wearing a dark suit and tie, speaking seriously in front of a 'CARLING CUP' backdrop with visible microphones, as the Narrator mentions 'Mourinho's shithousery master...'.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins by dispelling the notion that José Mourinho's confrontational tactics at Real Madrid started with a carpark incident, instead detailing his established 'unique management style' and the team's 4-0 Champions League victory over Ajax, which rendered their final group game against Auxerre a 'dead rubber' and secured their Round of 16 qualification. The strategy, as explained by the Narrator, involved players intentionally receiving yellow cards to clear suspensions for the knockout stages, a common but often discreet practice. However, Mourinho's approach, involving Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos, was deemed 'sneaky yet clever' but ultimately 'too far' and 'obvious,' leading to their bookings for time-wasting. This blatant tactic, despite attempts at discretion like substitute goalkeeper Jerzy Dudek relaying messages to Iker Casillas and Sergio Ramos, and Cristiano Ronaldo also receiving a yellow card, was noticed by UEFA, who launched an investigation. Consequently, UEFA found all involved guilty, imposing financial sanctions: Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos were fined €20,000 each, while other players, including Iker Casillas, faced a €10,000 fine, as highlighted by on-screen text and the Narrator's concluding remarks.

On-Screen Text (Match Result).description: White text overlays such as 'REAL MADRID SLAMMED AJAX 4-0 IN THE UCL GROUP STAGE PENULTIMATE GAME' and 'SECURED THEIR PLACE ON THE ROUND OF 16', visually reinforcing the narrator's spoken account of Real Madrid's victory over Ajax and their advancement in the Champions League.

The Narrator explicitly states he was part of Mourinho's plan to get booked, was sent off for time wasting, and was fined €20,000 by UEFA.

raw_segments: The video opens with a dramatic silhouette of a man walking through a carpark at sunset, overlaid with bold white text reading 'CONFRONTING REFEREES AT THE CARPARK', as the narrator's voice begins, stating that "Jose Mourinho at Real Madrid didn't start by confronting referees at the carpark." The scene transitions to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, in his studio with football memorabilia, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures, explaining that "the special one had already made headlines for his unique management style." He introduces a story about José Mourinho’s intense interactions with referees, specifically referencing Real Madrid players being sent off, urging viewers to "Let me explain because I already see many of you getting really confused just hearing this." The video cuts to archival footage of Real Madrid players in white jerseys celebrating a 4-0 victory over Ajax in the Champions League group stage, with on-screen text 'REAL MADRID SLAMMED AJAX 4-0 IN THE UCL GROUP STAGE PENULTIMATE GAME', which the narrator confirms, stating "Real Madrid smashed Ajax 4-0 in the Champions League group stage penultimate game." The Narrator continues, explaining how this result "essentially made their last match against Auxerre a total dead rubber game," as Real Madrid "secured their first place ticket to the Round of 16," reinforced by a graphic overlay stating 'SECURED THEIR PLACE ON THE ROUND OF 16'. He then details the Champions League yellow card rules, noting that "many coaches advise their own footballers to get booked before a dead rubber game." The video then shows a dramatic AI-reenactment of a referee holding a yellow card, with players reacting, followed by a close-up of Real Madrid player Sergio Ramos being shown a yellow card, as the narrator reveals that "Jose Mourinho isn't like other coaches" and took this "sneaky yet clever strategy a bit too far," with "Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos" having already picked up a yellow card. The scene shifts to a dejected Mourinho on the bench, with text overlay 'LOSING THEM FOR THE FIRST LEG OF THE LAST-16', highlighting the consequence that "getting booked against Auxerre would mean losing them for the first leg of the last 16." Throughout, a consistent, slightly dramatic background music plays, complementing the dynamic flow between the studio narration and archival/reenactment footage, with bold text overlays emphasizing key points.

The video opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache, in his studio adorned with football jerseys and a world map, speaking directly to the camera with expressive hand gestures. On-screen text dramatically declares, 'WHAT CAME NEXT WAS AS GENIUS AS OUTRAGEOUS,' as the Narrator's voice explains, "What came next was as genius as it was outrageous." He continues, detailing how "the special one" (José Mourinho, seen agitated on the sidelines in archival footage) spoke to Xabi Alonso and instructed substitute goalkeeper Jerzy Dudek to relay a message to Iker Casillas (seen reacting with frustration) and Sergio Ramos. The Narrator clarifies the "simple" plan: "get booked again and therefore sent off, to not carry the booking into the next round." The scene then shows Cristiano Ronaldo confronting a referee and receiving a yellow card. The Narrator reappears, commenting that "the Real Madrid stars were not as discreet as they should have been," echoing the on-screen text 'NOT AS DISCRETE AS THEY SHOULD HAVE BEEN,' because "their plan was so obvious that everyone saw it." A woman with glasses holding a tablet displaying a player’s ID is shown, followed by Sergio Ramos in a white Real Madrid jersey receiving a yellow card. The Narrator's voice confirms that "Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos got sent off for time wasting," with the defender even shaking the referee's hand. After a brief shot of players shaking hands on a training ground, the Narrator gestures emphatically as a man with a magnifying glass appears with the text 'UEFA PROCEEDED TO INVESTIGATE THE MATTER.' The Narrator's voice confirms, "UEFA proceeded to investigate the matter and logically found all of them guilty." This is followed by a dramatic cutaway of a bearded man in an orange cap, whose voice is heard expressing exaggerated shock, "Oh no! What, what happened? How terrible!" The Narrator then announces, "Imposing a financial sanction to all of the players involved." The final frames show split-screen footage: on the left, a player in a white jersey is tackled during a match, with '€20,000' overlaid, as the Narrator states, "Players Xabi Alonso and Sergio Ramos were fined €20,000 each." On the right, a man in a suit speaks to reporters, with microphones from 'europapress.es' and 'me' visible. The segment concludes with a man in a white shirt speaking into a microphone at a press conference, with '€10,000' overlaid against a 'HYUNDAI' and 'SANTAFE' backdrop, as the Narrator's audio cuts off while mentioning Iker Casillas.

event_blocks.summary: The Narrator details financial penalties and suspensions, stating CS was fined €10,000 and Jerzy Dudek $5,000, while Alonso and Ramos received one-match suspensions for their red cards, and Real Madrid was fined €120,000, a decision deemed a good deal for the club and especially for Mourinho, who was also suspended but had players available for the round of 16. Archival footage shows a man with money and celebrating fans, as the Narrator recalls Mourinho's high expectations after winning the Champions League at Inter, followed by scenes of trophy ceremonies and parades, before concluding that the Portuguese coach left Madrid after three seasons with domestic titles but no international success. The Narrator then explains that Mourinho achieved a different victory by dragging Guardiola into the mud and lowering the Catalan to his turf. At a press conference with a UEFA Champions League sponsor backdrop, José Mourinho, in a dark suit, speaks in Spanish, 'Pero ha ganado una Champions que a mí me daría vergüenza de ganarla,' translated as 'HE'S WON A CHAMPIONS THAT I'D BE ASHAMED OF WINNING.' In response, Pep Guardiola, in a blue tracksuit, appears at his own press conference and begins speaking in Spanish, 'Sé cuál es la cámara del señor...,' with the on-screen text translating to 'I DON'T KNOW WHICH IS JOSE'S CAMERA, IT MUST BE ALL OF THEM,' highlighting their intense rivalry.

José Mourinho.description: Appears in a dark suit and tie, speaking at a press conference with a backdrop of UEFA Champions League sponsors, with a serious expression, indicating his role as a manager under pressure, and is heard speaking in Spanish with a distinct accent, stating, "Pero ha ganado una Champions que a mí me daría vergüenza de ganarla."

Studio Backdrop.description: Features a world map, hanging football jerseys, and decorative plants, creating a thematic setting for the narration, with warm lighting focused on the Narrator.

Narrator.description: A man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio with football memorabilia in the background, speaking directly to the camera and gesturing as he narrates in a clear, informative voice, providing commentary and context throughout the segment.

On-Screen Text (Press Conference Quotes).description: Red text overlays during Mourinho’s press conference, quoting his statements: 'HE'S WON A CHAMPIONS THAT I'D BE ASHAMED OF WINNING.' and 'I DON'T KNOW WHICH IS JOSE'S CAMERA, IT MUST BE ALL OF THEM,' emphasizing the rivalry and controversy.

raw_segments: The video opens with a man in a black tracksuit walking through a tunnel, as the Narrator states that "CS was fined €10,000 and Jerzy Dudek was fined $5,000," with a graphic overlay of '€5,000' appearing, suggesting a financial penalty. The Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, then appears in a studio with football memorabilia, explaining that "the red cards given to Alonso and Ramos stand, and they have therefore been suspended for one UEFA Club competition match." He continues, detailing how "the control and disciplinary body has decided to fine Real Madrid €120,000," adding that "it was a pretty good deal for Real Madrid, and especially for Mourinho," who "was suspended as well, but he had his stars ready and without bookings for the round of 16." Archival footage then shows a man in a suit holding a stack of money with a celebrating crowd in white tracksuits, as the Narrator recounts Mourinho's high expectations "after winning the Champions League at Inter." The scene shifts to a trophy ceremony with confetti and a '32 LIGAS' banner, then a parade float with 'CAMPEONES' and cheering fans, before the Narrator reappears to conclude that "after three seasons in which he won La Liga, the Copa del Rey and the Spanish Cup, but without an international success, the Portuguese coach left Madrid." A brief transition with sound effects leads back to the Narrator, who states that "Mourinho got a different victory out of this," having "essentially dragged Guardiola to the mud" and "lowered the Catalan to his turf." José Mourinho, in a dark suit, is then seen at a press conference with a UEFA Champions League sponsor backdrop, speaking in Spanish, "Pero ha ganado una Champions que a mí me daría vergüenza de ganarla," which is translated by the on-screen text as 'HE'S WON A CHAMPIONS THAT I'D BE ASHAMED OF WINNING.' The Narrator introduces Pep's reply, and Pep Guardiola, in a blue tracksuit, appears at a press conference, beginning to speak in Spanish, "Sé cuál es la cámara del señor...", with the text overlay 'I DON'T KNOW WHICH IS JOSE'S CAMERA, IT MUST BE ALL OF THEM,' emphasizing the rivalry.

event_blocks.summary: Pep Guardiola, speaking Spanish at a press conference, declared José Mourinho 'the f*cking boss, the f*cking master, the one who knows the most in the world,' expressing no desire to compete with him. The Narrator, in his memorabilia-filled studio, explained that Pep was furious and that Mourinho's objective was to 'destroy Barcelona,' after which he left the club. A comedic cutaway featured Homer Simpson sweating under the sun, exclaiming about the heat. The Narrator then reappeared, referencing 'ALWAYS: THE SPECIAL ONE,' as archival footage showed Mourinho returning to Chelsea FC in 2013, described as wiser. Later, the Narrator detailed Mourinho's 'unique and controversial personality' and 'public fights,' his 'tormentous, title-less first season,' and failure to elevate Chelsea. During a match, on-screen text noted 'SLOWLY GIVING THE BALL TO CESAR AZPILICUETA' and 'NEEDED TO SPEED UP THEIR ATTACKS' as a Chelsea player was tackled. The Narrator stated, 'He's out of line, but he's right,' as Mourinho was seen pushing a ball boy who was delaying returning the ball to Azpilicueta, with Mourinho later quoted saying, 'I THOUGHT CESAR AZPILICUETA WAS GOING TO LOSE EMOTIONAL CONTROL (WITH THE BALLBOY)' and warning the boy about potential retaliation. The Narrator then referenced 'Hazard's incident one year before, where he kicked a ball boy at Swansea for similar reasons,' adding, 'Poor Eden, he had nothing to do with it.' The Narrator further explained Mourinho's perceived 'untouchable' status after escaping FA sanctions, leading to a confrontation with Arsène Wenger where Mourinho called him 'a specialist in failure.' Wenger retaliated, and they had to be separated. The segment concluded with Mourinho, in a Chelsea jersey celebrating in the rain and later walking through fans, and a final tense face-to-face with Wenger, leaving the Narrator to ponder who would have won a hypothetical fist fight.

José Mourinho.description: Appears in multiple scenes: first in a dark suit confronting Arsène Wenger on the sidelines, then in a blue jacket at a press conference with sponsor logos behind him where his own voice is heard stating, "You know, he is a specialist in failure. Uh, I'm not," and later in a blue Chelsea jersey with 'LAMPARD 8' on the back celebrating in the rain, and finally in a suit at a Chelsea press conference with a serious expression.

Homer Simpson.description: A yellow-skinned animated character with glasses, wearing a white shirt and blue tie, sweating profusely under the sun, with the text 'A VERY HOT DAY IN BARCELONA' overlaid. He speaks, saying, 'Well, you're certainly doing your job today, Mr. Sun,' and 'Oh, rats.'

Pep Guardiola.description: Appears in a blue tracksuit with a black collar, speaking at a press conference with a serious expression, against a backdrop of UEFA Champions League sponsors. He speaks in Spanish, stating, 'José... he is the f*cking boss, the f*cking master, he is the one who knows the most in the world. I don't want to compete for an instant,' with red text overlays quoting his remarks about José Mourinho.

Narrator.description: A man with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio with football memorabilia including a world map and hanging jerseys, speaking directly to the camera and gesturing as he narrates in a clear, informative voice, providing context and commentary throughout the segment.

raw_segments: The minute opens with Pep Guardiola, dressed in a blue tracksuit, speaking intensely and seriously at a press conference against a backdrop of UEFA Champions League sponsors. He declares in Spanish, with red text overlays quoting his remarks, that José is 'the f*cking boss, the f*cking master, the one who knows the most in the world,' and that he doesn't wish to compete with him for an instant. The scene then cuts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, in his studio filled with football memorabilia. With dramatic background music, he explains that 'Pep was fuming, we never saw him like that again and shortly after their duels are over, the club legend left Barcelona. Mourinho had done what he was brought in to do all along, to destroy Barcelona.' A loud air horn then introduces a comedic cutaway of Homer Simpson sweating profusely under the sun, with 'A VERY HOT DAY IN BARCELONA' text, as Homer's voice exclaims, 'Well, you're certainly doing your job today, Mr. Sun,' followed by 'Oh, rats.' The Narrator reappears, holding a card reading 'ALWAYS: THE SPECIAL ONE,' before archival footage shows José Mourinho in a dark suit, walking with another man, with 'JOSÉ RETURNED TO CHELSEA FC' displayed. The Narrator continues, 'London was calling once again, more specifically, the blue side of London. In 2013, six years after his departure, Jose returned to Chelsea. He was wiser, but...' as the dramatic music persists and he gestures expressively in his warmly lit studio.

The video opens with a press conference featuring two Chelsea players in blue jerseys with 'SAMSUNG' sponsorship, standing before a backdrop filled with logos, while a man in a grey sweater gestures. A male Narrator, with reddish hair and a mustache, wearing a brown t-shirt in his studio with football memorabilia, immediately begins speaking animatedly into a microphone, detailing José Mourinho's "unique and controversial personality" and his history of "public fights." The Narrator continues, explaining Mourinho's "tormentous, title-less first season" and how he "failed to take Chelsea to the next level." The scene then cuts to a match where a Chelsea player in a blue jersey is tackled by an opponent in a black and red kit, with on-screen text stating 'SLOWLY GIVING THE BALL TO CESAR AZPILICUETA' and 'NEEDED TO SPEED UP THEIR ATTACKS'. The Narrator's voice returns, stating, "He's out of line, but he's right," as the visual shifts to a sideline incident where a man in a black coat, identified as José Mourinho, pushes a young ball boy in a red jacket. The Narrator explains that the youngster was "slowly giving the ball back to Cesar Azpilicueta" and that "the Blues clearly needed to speed up their attacks." Mourinho, described as "controversial as usual," is then seen in a white jacket at a press conference, with on-screen text quoting him: 'I THOUGHT CESAR AZPILICUETA WAS GOING TO LOSE EMOTIONAL CONTROL (WITH THE BALLBOY)'. Mourinho's own distinct male voice is heard confirming, "I thought Cesar Azpilicueta was going to lose emotional control," and adding, "I had the chance to get to the kid and I told him, you do this one day and somebody punches you." The Narrator reappears, gesturing, and then the video shows a dramatic match moment where a Chelsea player in blue kicks an opponent on the ground, followed by a swirling transition. The Narrator concludes by referencing "Hazard's incident one year before, where he kicked a ball boy at Swansea for similar reasons," adding, "Poor Eden, he had nothing to do with it."

The segment opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio adorned with a world map, hanging football jerseys, and plants. He speaks directly to the camera with expressive gestures, his clear voice introducing the topic by stating, "the special one spares no man. Mourinho escaped the FA with no sanction, so he was feeling pretty untouchable," while on-screen text displays 'Real Madrid The Special One' and 'PRETTY UNTOUCHABLE'. The Narrator continues, explaining, "if you thought that that was the last problematic Mourinho episode at Chelsea, you couldn't be more wrong. 2014 was also the year in which Mourinho and Arsène Wenger almost got into a fist fight." This transitions to archival footage showing José Mourinho in a dark suit confronting Arsène Wenger in a grey tracksuit on the sidelines, with on-screen text 'ARSENE WENGER ALMOST GOT INTO A FIST FIGHT'. Mourinho is seen pushing Wenger, who reacts with a raised hand, as a crowd watches. The video returns to the Narrator, who continues his animated narration, describing the confrontation as "one of the most bizarre and at the same time expected confrontations," explaining that "Mourinho called his colleague a specialist in failure for not having brought silverware to the Emirates in eight years." This leads to a clip of Mourinho in a blue jacket at a press conference, with sponsor logos like Samsung, Audi, and Gazprom visible behind him, where his own voice is heard stating, "You know, he is a specialist in failure. Uh, I'm not," accompanied by the on-screen text 'YOU KNOW, HE'S A SPECIALIST IN FAILURE.' The Narrator's voice continues, describing Wenger as "visibly outraged" and how he "shoved the Chelsea boss who retaliated in return." Visually, the scene shifts to Mourinho in a blue Chelsea jersey with 'LAMPARD 8' on the back, celebrating in the rain with arms raised, followed by him in a suit, walking through a crowd of fans, shaking hands and smiling. A close-up then shows him facing Wenger again in a tense face-to-face confrontation. The Narrator concludes, stating, "The coaches had to be separated and the match was temporarily stopped. Although I can't help but wonder who would have prevailed in a hypothetical fist fight between the two." The final shot shows Mourinho at a Chelsea press conference, standing at a podium with the club’s logo and sponsor logos, looking down with a serious expression as he prepares to speak, with the Narrator's voice fading out.

event_blocks.summary: José Mourinho's tenure at Chelsea was marked by his "outrageous personality" dominating headlines, even during successful seasons where the Blues won the League Cup and Premier League. This time, however, the "first punch" came not from Mourinho, but from Rafa Benítez's wife, Montserrat Serra, who claimed her husband had to "clean up after Mourinho's Real Madrid," implying Mourinho's teams were messes. Mourinho, in response, questioned Serra's knowledge and pointed out that Benítez had replaced him at Chelsea, Real Madrid, and Inter Milan, but not always directly, as other managers often preceded Benítez. He specifically stated that Benítez replaced Roberto Di Matteo at Chelsea and Carlo Ancelotti at Real Madrid, with Inter Milan being the only club where Benítez directly succeeded him, and there, Benítez "destroyed the best team in Europe." The narrative then shifts to Mourinho's public criticism of Chelsea's physio, Eva Carneiro, after she ran onto the pitch to treat Eden Hazard during a match where Chelsea was "down to 10 men." Mourinho, infuriated, stated he "wasn't happy with my medical staff" and that Carneiro should have been sure a player had a "serious problem." He further explained that his medical staff left him with "eight fit outfield players." Despite player support for Carneiro, she left the club and took the matter to court. Mourinho admitted to calling her "the daughter of a bitch" in Portuguese, but defended it as a common football phrase, akin to "f*** off," with no sexist connotation, a defense echoed in press conferences and on-screen text. Chelsea offered a $1.5 million settlement, which was rejected, and the case was settled out of court nearly a year later, after both Mourinho and Carneiro had left the club. Mourinho subsequently joined Manchester United, leading them to their best era since Sir Alex Ferguson's departure, winning multiple titles including the Europa League, League Cup, and Community Shield, and achieving a second-place Premier League finish. His relationship with Paul Pogba became strained, particularly after an injury, with Mourinho reportedly sending a photo of Pogba and his wife to agent Mino Raiola, questioning Pogba's commitment. Pogba confronted Mourinho, stating, "Are you serious? I'm injured. I train three times a day here." Mourinho eventually left Manchester United six months before his contract expired, with a comedic cutaway depicting his "spectacular coaching career" engulfed in flames.

raw_segments: The video opens with a dynamic montage of football action, showing Chelsea players in blue kits walking onto the pitch, followed by a close-up of a player being tackled. A male narrator's clear, informative voice begins, explaining, "Mourinho's first year back at London, but in his second season, even though the Blues won the League Cup and the Premier League, his outrageous personality continued to dominate headlines." The scene quickly shifts to José Mourinho in a blue Chelsea tracksuit, seated at a press conference table with sponsor logos like Samsung, Audi, and Gazprom behind him. The narrator continues, "Although this time, it wasn't Jose who threw the first punch." The narrative then cuts to the Narrator in a studio setting, standing in front of a backdrop featuring a world map and hanging football jerseys, speaking directly to the camera. The video transitions to a split-screen showing Mourinho in a suit at a press conference and a woman with blonde hair, identified by text overlay as 'RAFA BENÍTEZ'S WIFE TO MARIA DE MONTSERRAT'. The Narrator's voice introduces her, "Rafa Benítez's wife Montserrat Serra said that her husband had to clean up after Mourinho's Real Madrid are the third of Jose Mourinho's old teams Rafa has coached." The scene changes to Real Madrid players training in grey and yellow kits, with text explaining Rafa Benítez's coaching history. The Narrator quotes, "We tidy up his messes," then questions, "Did this woman even know who she was poking?" The video then shows Mourinho in a suit at a Chelsea press conference, with text overlay 'KNOW WHO WAS POKING'. The Narrator explains, "It was partially true that Benítez had replaced Mourinho at Chelsea, Inter and Real Madrid. However, there were other managers in between Rafa and Jose." The Narrator reappears, emphasizing the theme with text 'DON'T MESS WITH THE SPECIAL ONE', warning, "But still, don't mess with the special one or he'll bite back." The video cuts to Mourinho in a suit, sitting on the grass, looking dejected, as the Narrator describes, "Mourinho, who had a bitter rival with Benítez since the Spaniard was Liverpool's manager, when the Reds knocked Chelsea out of the UCL semis twice..." Liverpool players are then shown celebrating in red kits with text 'WHEN THE REDS KNOCKED CHELSEA OUT OF THE UCL SEMI-FINALS'. The Narrator continues, "...trashed the woman and seized the opportunity to mess with his colleague." The Narrator returns, gesturing as he speaks, then the video shows Mourinho at a press conference with text overlay 'THE LADY IS A BIT CONFUSED. HER HUSBAND WENT TO CHELSEA TO REPLACE ROBERTO DI MATTEO AND HE WENT TO REAL MADRID AND REPLACED CARLO ANCELOTTI.' Mourinho's distinct, confident, and slightly accented voice is heard directly, stating, "The lady is a bit confused. Her husband went to Chelsea to replace Roberto Di Matteo, and he went to Real Madrid and replaced Carlo Ancelotti." The final scene shows Mourinho in a suit, walking away from a press conference with text 'THE ONLY CLUB WHERE HER HUSBAND REPLACED ME WAS AT INTER MILAN, WHERE IN SIX MONTHS HE DESTROYED THE BEST TEAM IN EUROPE AT THE TIME.' Mourinho's voice concludes, "The only club where her husband replaced me was at Inter Milan, where in six months he destroyed the best team in Europe at the time." The video maintains a fast-paced, dramatic tone, using text overlays to emphasize key points and transitions between scenes to build narrative tension.

The minute opens with the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, standing in a studio with a world map and football jerseys, speaking directly to the camera with expressive gestures, his clear voice discussing Mourinho's past achievements and transitioning to a segment on 'unnecessary criticism.' The scene transitions to archival footage of José Mourinho on a training ground, walking among players, as on-screen text introduces 'AND SPEAKING OF UNNECESSARY CRITICISM...'. The video then cuts to a press conference where Mourinho, in a black Chelsea tracksuit, delivers a pointed statement in his distinct, accented voice, heard saying, "And for her also to think about me and to speak about me, I think the lady needs to occupy her time, and if she takes care of her husband's diet, she will have less time to speak about me," with these exact words appearing as red text overlays. The Narrator's voice returns, commenting on Mourinho's "not too classy" remark about "Rafa's weight." The video then shows Mourinho walking off the pitch after a match, followed by celebratory footage of Chelsea players lifting the Premier League trophy, with on-screen text proclaiming 'CHELSEA'S PREMIER LEAGUE TITLE' and 'SEEMED TO BE THE FIRST STEP OF ANOTHER GOLDEN ERA,' as the Narrator describes this period. The final scenes include Mourinho in a suit walking away from a press conference, and a tense moment where he confronts a woman in a blue Chelsea kit, identified by on-screen text as 'MOURINHO PUBLICLY CRITICIZED CHELSEA'S PHYSIO EVA CARNEIRO,' a name the Narrator pronounces as "Eva Carnera," explaining the incident occurred during a match where the team was "down to 10 men," followed by a blurred shot of a player receiving a red card from a referee.

The video opens with a dramatic, fiery visual effect as the Narrator's voice sets the scene, explaining Courtois' red card and Eden Hazard's physical problem. Visually, José Mourinho, in a dark suit, walks on the pitch with a stern, upset expression, while Eva Carneiro, in a blue Chelsea kit, runs urgently toward a player, an action the Narrator states "infuriated Mourinho." Mourinho's angry, indistinct shouts are heard amidst stadium crowd noise as he walks away from the sideline. The scene then cuts to a press conference where Mourinho, now in a black and blue Chelsea tracksuit, speaks into a cluster of microphones with a serious, frustrated demeanor. His voice is heard stating, "I wasn't happy with my medical staff. If you are a medical doctor on the bench, you have to understand the game. If you go to the pitch to assist a player, then you must be sure that a player has a serious problem. I was sure that Eden didn't have a serious problem. He had a knock and was very tired." On-screen red text overlays these quotes, and he further explains that his "medical department left me with eight fit outfield players in a counter-attack after a set piece, and we were worried we didn't have enough players left." The video then shifts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, speaking directly to the camera from a studio with football memorabilia. The Narrator continues, detailing Mourinho's statement admitting to insulting her by calling her "the daughter of a bitch" in his language. The scene returns to Mourinho at the press conference, where on-screen text quotes him saying "'FILHO DA P*TA' IS A PHRASE I OFTEN USE, ALL OF THE PLAYERS KNOW IT. THERE IS NO SEXIST CONNOTATION IN THE USE OF THE PHRASE - IT IS JUST LIKE SAYING 'F*** OFF'. IN THE WORLD OF FOOTBALL, A LOT OF SWEAR WORDS ARE USED." Mourinho's voice is heard saying "Filho da p*ta" and elaborating on his perspective, emphasizing the context of football language, before the Narrator concludes by summarizing the incident's public fallout and legal action.

The segment opens with José Mourinho, wearing a red Manchester United tracksuit with 'JM' on the chest, seated seriously before MUTV microphones against a backdrop of club and Adidas logos, stating, "I want him to play well. I want him to play for the team. I want him to play for the fans. And that's what he did. I have no problems at all." On-screen text simultaneously quotes him: 'I WANT HIM TO PLAY WELL, I WANT HIM TO PLAY FOR THE TEAM, I WANT HIM TO PLAY FOR THE FANS AND THAT’S WHAT IT IS.' The scene then cuts to the Narrator, a man with reddish hair and a mustache in a brown t-shirt, gesturing expressively in his studio, which features a world map and hanging football jerseys. A comedic cutaway then shows a cartoon Mourinho engulfed in flames, labeled 'SPECTACULAR COACHING CAREER,' as the Narrator concludes, "But we all know the special one and he wouldn't leave Manchester United without bragging about his spectacular coaching career, setting his colleagues on fire at the same time."

event_blocks.summary: José Mourinho, initially at a press conference with Manchester United and Adidas logos, emphatically declared his three Premier League titles, stating he had won more than the other 19 managers combined, a claim highlighted by on-screen text. The scene shifted to the Narrator in a studio, explaining Mourinho's managerial hiatus due to group management issues before joining Tottenham, where he became a central figure in their Amazon series, delivering demanding speeches. Later, on a grassy training ground, Mourinho, in a purple puffer jacket, intensely motivated players in similar gear, urging them to be "intelligent c*nts, not stupid c*nts" and "b*stards" to win matches, a statement reinforced by on-screen text. The Narrator then discussed Mourinho's struggles with Tottenham's "constant irregularity" and players not meeting expectations, showing training footage and text overlays. A press conference clip featured Dele Alli, before whom Mourinho offered career advice. Returning to the training ground, Mourinho, in a black puffer jacket, observed players as the Narrator recited Mourinho's critique of Alli's "ups and downs" and the difference between "a top, top player and a player with top, top potential." The Narrator reiterated this analysis applied to Mourinho's Tottenham, noting their alternation between "fantastic games and incredibly poor performances," with on-screen text confirming this inconsistency. The segment concluded with a focus on Dele Alli's inconsistent form, attributed by the Narrator to "poor recruitment and disbelief in Jose's idea of waiting and counter-attacking."
